concerns.text: FR-006 mandates 'multiple-comparison correction... if more than 3 features are tested' but anchors to US-3. US-3 (SHAP Analysis) focuses on identifying top 3 features and generating plots, not on statistical hypothesis testing of feature significance. The requirement scope (statistical correction) does not clearly match the story scope (interpretation/visualization). Re-anchor to a story that explicitly covers statistical validation or clarify the story to include this statistical step.

SC-005 requires measuring 'false discovery rate (FDR) of the top feature rankings' against a threshold. This success criterion relies on the statistical testing implied in FR-006. Since FR-006's anchor to US-3 is tenuous (US-3 is about SHAP plots, not FDR testing), SC-005 inherits this orphan risk. Ensure the story explicitly covers statistical significance testing of features, or re-anchor SC-005 to a story that does.
